title: AG: Mass. to receive $730,000 from Cash App in alleged deception settlement
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts will receive $730,000 from Block Inc. in a Cash App settlement over misleading security claims.
Block allegedly failed to warn users about rising fraud on Cash App, impacting users more vulnerable without bank accounts.
The settlement mandates improved user protections, accurate safety claims, and responsive customer support from Block.

title: Tyngsborough man may face charges after allegedly taking police boat, posing as officer
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A 63-year-old man allegedly took a Tyngsborough police boat onto Lake Mascuppic, activating its emergency lights.
Authorities say the man attempted to enforce local boating rules and ordered people to leave the lake.
Tyngsborough police, with other agencies, plan to summon him for impersonating a police officer in Lowell District Court.

title: Photos: Stunning views of Sail Boston 250
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Over 60 international tall ships and military vessels arrived in Boston for the six-day Sail Boston 2026 event.
The USS Constitution led the Parade of Sail, representing more than 20 countries, into Boston Harbor on Saturday.
Thousands of visitors gathered at various Boston waterfront locations to witness the final stop of the Sail250 tour.

title: Red Sox draft SS Jake Schaffner with No. 20 pick
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox select shortstop Jake Schaffner from University of North Carolina with the No. 20 pick in 2026 MLB Draft.
Schaffner hit .356 with six home runs, 50 RBI, and 26 steals during UNC's College World Series run.
Defensively solid with .987 fielding percentage, Schaffner may shift to second base due to arm strength.

title: Sail Boston 2026: The Grand Parade of Sail is rooted in rich history and tradition in the Commonwealth
author: Laurie Fullerton
bullets: The 2026 Sail Boston Grand Parade of Sail is led by the USS Constitution, showcasing historic ships.
The event features ships like the Grand Banks replica schooner Bluenose Two, celebrating maritime history.
Many spectators, including families and past residents, attended to honor tradition and engage with history.

title: Brayan Bello named midseason AL ‘Cy Yuk’ winner by The Athletic for poor first-half performance
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Brayan Bello received The Athletic's 'Cy Yuk' award for his poor first-half performance with a 10.35 ERA.
Bello allowed 41 earned runs over 35.2 innings with a WHIP of 2.21 in eight Red Sox starts.
Despite excelling in relief with a 0.71 ERA, the Red Sox sent Bello to Triple-A rather than promoting him.

title: Red Sox face all too familiar question heading into MLB trade deadline: Buy or sell?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: The Boston Red Sox face a dilemma at the 2026 trade deadline: consider a playoff push or sell players.
Boston's recent seven-game winning streak puts it within two games of an American League wild-card spot.
General Manager Craig Breslow must decide to buy or sell, affecting both his future and the team's direction.

title: Review & Setlist: Shakira delivers high-energy nostalgia and new beginnings at TD Garden
author: Maddie Browning
bullets: Shakira returned to music with 'Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran' after a seven-year hiatus, winning a 2025 Grammy.
Shakira's concert at TD Garden featured a mix of nostalgia and new themes, celebrating resilience and empowerment.
The show included hits like 'Hips Don't Lie' and 'Whenever, Wherever,' with a performance honoring the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

title: Healey announces tax credit awards to build almost 700 new homes in six Mass. cities
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: The Healey-Driscoll administration announced $18.6 million in tax credits to build 662 new homes in six Massachusetts cities.
The Housing Development Incentive Program funds redevelopment in Fall River, Lawrence, Lowell, Pittsfield, Taunton, and Worcester.
Projects include converting historic buildings and mills, with tax credits ranging from $1.5M to $4M per project.

title: FBI arrests Mass. man in armed bank robberies in NH, NJ
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Joseph Sawyer, 52, from Townsend, MA, was arrested by a SWAT team in Albany, New York, for two armed bank robberies.
Sawyer allegedly robbed banks in Nashua, NH, and Boonton, NJ, in June, stealing a total of $18,000.
The arrest was part of the FBI's Operation Summer Heat 2.0, involving multiple state and federal law enforcement agencies.
